timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Ivan IV marries Anastasia Romanovna
description: Ivan IV married Anastasia Romanovna Zakharina-Yureva, linking the future Romanov family to the ruling house of Muscovy.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Death of Ivan IV
description: The death of Ivan IV weakened the political world from which the Romanovs would later emerge as Russia's new ruling dynasty.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: End of the Rurik dynasty
description: Tsar Fyodor I died without an heir, ending the Rurik line and helping trigger the Time of Troubles.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Michael Romanov is elected tsar
description: A national assembly chose Michael Romanov as tsar, founding the Romanov dynasty.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Aleksey succeeds Michael
description: Tsar Aleksey inherited the throne and consolidated Romanov rule during a long reign.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Peter I becomes co-tsar
description: Peter I became co-ruler of Russia, beginning the career of the most transformative Romanov ruler.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Peter I takes power
description: Peter I displaced the regency of Sophia and assumed effective control of the government.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Saint Petersburg is founded
description: Peter I founded Saint Petersburg, which became a symbol of imperial modernization and westward orientation.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Russia becomes an empire
description: After victory in the Great Northern War, Peter proclaimed the Russian Empire and took the title emperor.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Death of Peter the Great
description: Peter's death opened a prolonged era of palace politics and disputed succession within the Romanov house.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Catherine II seizes the throne
description: Catherine II overthrew Peter III and began one of the most influential reigns in Romanov history.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: First partition of Poland
description: Russia took major territory in the first partition of Poland, reflecting Romanov imperial expansion under Catherine II.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Paul I succeeds Catherine II
description: Paul I became emperor after Catherine's death and introduced a new succession law for the dynasty.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Napoleon invades Russia
description: The French invasion became the defining military crisis of Alexander I's reign and a turning point in European politics.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Decembrist revolt erupts
description: Army officers revolted at the accession of Nicholas I, revealing elite opposition to autocracy.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Emancipation of the serfs
description: Alexander II issued the emancipation reform that legally freed millions of serfs across the empire.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Alexander II is assassinated
description: The assassination of Alexander II ended the great reforming reign and hardened the dynasty's political course.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Nicholas II becomes emperor
description: Nicholas II inherited the throne as the last Romanov emperor.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Revolution of 1905 forces concessions
description: Military defeat and unrest pushed Nicholas II to issue the October Manifesto and create the Duma.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, reform, and collapse of the Romanov dynasty that ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917.
title: Nicholas II abdicates
description: Nicholas II abdicated during the February Revolution, ending Romanov rule after more than three centuries.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Sri Gupta establishes the family line
description: Sri Gupta is the earliest known ruler of the Gupta line and marks the beginning of the dynasty's rise.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Ghatotkacha succeeds Sri Gupta
description: Ghatotkacha ruled as an early Gupta chief before the dynasty became an imperial power.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Chandragupta I begins his reign
description: Chandragupta I is generally regarded as the founder of the Gupta Empire.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Marriage alliance with the Licchavis
description: The marriage of Chandragupta I and Kumaradevi strengthened Gupta legitimacy and regional power.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Samudragupta succeeds Chandragupta I
description: Samudragupta inherited the throne and began the empire's major phase of expansion.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Northern campaigns expand Gupta power
description: Samudragupta defeated rival rulers across northern India and established imperial dominance.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Allahabad pillar inscription is issued
description: The inscription celebrated Samudragupta's conquests and remains a major source for Gupta history.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Chandragupta II comes to power
description: Chandragupta II succeeded to the throne and presided over the empire's classical florescence.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Western Kshatrapas are defeated
description: Gupta victory over the Western Kshatrapas extended control to western India and seacoast trade routes.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Fa-hsien visits Gupta India
description: The Chinese Buddhist pilgrim Fa-hsien recorded prosperity and relative stability under Gupta rule.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Ujjain emerges as a major center
description: Under Chandragupta II, Ujjain became an important imperial and commercial center.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Kumaragupta I succeeds
description: Kumaragupta I inherited the empire and preserved Gupta strength for much of the fifth century.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Nalanda gains royal patronage
description: Nalanda developed into a major center of Buddhist learning during the later Gupta era.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Skandagupta defeats invading Huns
description: Skandagupta repelled early Huna incursions, though at growing military and fiscal cost.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Death of Skandagupta
description: After Skandagupta's death, the empire entered a more fragile phase of fragmentation.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Huna pressure intensifies
description: Renewed Huna invasions weakened Gupta authority across northern India.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Imperial territory contracts sharply
description: By around 500, Gupta power had shrunk and regional kingdoms were asserting independence.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Toramana overruns Gupta lands
description: The Alchon Hun ruler Toramana advanced into former Gupta territory during the empire's decline.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Mihirakula is defeated by Indian rulers
description: Coalitions of Indian rulers checked Huna expansion, but Gupta political unity was not restored.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, expansion, cultural flowering, and decline of the Gupta Empire in ancient India.
title: Gupta Empire comes to an end
description: By the mid-sixth century the Gupta Empire had effectively disappeared as a major imperial state.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Panic of 1907 shakes the banking system
description: The financial panic intensified calls for a central banking reform in the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Aldrich-Vreeland Act is enacted
description: Congress passed emergency currency legislation and created the National Monetary Commission.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Jekyll Island meeting takes place
description: Leading bankers and policy figures met privately to draft a framework for central bank reform.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Federal Reserve Act becomes law
description: President Woodrow Wilson signed the Federal Reserve Act, creating the Federal Reserve System.
source_publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: First Federal Reserve Board is sworn in
description: The first Federal Reserve Board formally began operations.
source_publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Reserve Banks open for business
description: The 12 regional Federal Reserve Banks opened and the new system became operational.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Fed supports wartime finance
description: During World War I the Federal Reserve played a major role in managing war finance and Treasury operations.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Open market coordination begins
description: The System created a committee to coordinate open market purchases among Reserve Banks.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Great Depression begins
description: The stock market crash ushered in the deepest crisis in Federal Reserve history.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Banking Act of 1933 reforms the system
description: Congress expanded federal banking reform during the Depression, including deposit insurance and structural changes.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Banking Act of 1935 strengthens the Board
description: The act reorganized the Federal Reserve System and created the modern Federal Open Market Committee.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Treasury-Fed Accord is reached
description: The accord restored greater Federal Reserve independence over monetary policy after World War II.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Modern inflation era begins
description: The Fed entered a period marked by inflation, unemployment pressures, and changing policy frameworks.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Volcker shifts anti-inflation policy
description: Chair Paul Volcker led a decisive tightening that made inflation control the Fed's central priority.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Fed responds to Black Monday
description: After the stock market crash, the Fed acted as a stabilizing lender and liquidity backstop.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: FOMC begins announcing rate decisions
description: The Federal Open Market Committee moved toward greater transparency by announcing policy actions.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Financial crisis begins
description: Stress in housing and credit markets triggered the most severe Fed crisis response since the Great Depression.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Emergency lending expands after Lehman
description: The Federal Reserve launched extraordinary facilities to stabilize markets during the global financial crisis.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Fed intervenes during the pandemic shock
description: The central bank cut rates, restarted large-scale asset purchases, and opened emergency facilities during the COVID-19 crisis.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Federal Reserve System
timeline_description: A timeline of the creation, evolution, and major turning points of the Federal Reserve System in the United States.
title: Rapid rate hikes target inflation surge
description: The Fed began an aggressive tightening cycle in response to the highest U.S. inflation in decades.
source_publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences is founded
description: The Academy was established in Hollywood, creating the institution that would organize the Oscars.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: First Academy Awards ceremony is held
description: The first Academy Awards ceremony took place at the Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Second ceremony introduces radio coverage
description: The second Oscars ceremony received a wider audience through live radio broadcasting.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Oscar nickname enters common use
description: By the sixth awards presentation, the nickname Oscar was already in public circulation.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Supporting acting awards are added
description: The Academy introduced awards for supporting actor and supporting actress.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Gone with the Wind wins Best Picture
description: The film's success marked one of the major early landmarks in Oscar history.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Hattie McDaniel wins Best Supporting Actress
description: McDaniel became the first African American performer to win an Academy Award.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Ceremony moves to Grauman's Chinese Theatre
description: The wartime-era ceremony reflected the changing scale and profile of the awards.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Hamlet wins Best Picture
description: Hamlet became the first non-Hollywood-financed film to win Best Picture.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: The Oscars are televised for the first time
description: Television transformed the ceremony into a national mass-media event.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Ceremony is first broadcast in color
description: The Academy Awards adapted to color television, reflecting changing broadcast technology.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Midnight Cowboy wins Best Picture
description: The film became the only X-rated winner of Best Picture.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: The Oscars move to the Dorothy Chandler Pavilion
description: The venue became closely associated with the ceremony for much of the late twentieth century.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: No host is used for the ceremony
description: The famously troubled 61st Academy Awards remained a reference point in Oscar history.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Ceremony moves to the Kodak Theatre
description: The new venue became the long-term home of the Academy Awards in Hollywood.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Best Picture field expands to ten nominees
description: The Academy broadened the Best Picture category after years of five nominees.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: #OscarsSoWhite controversy erupts
description: Debate over nominations intensified scrutiny of representation and Academy membership.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Parasite wins Best Picture
description: Parasite became the first non-English-language film to win Best Picture.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Oppenheimer wins Best Picture
description: Oppenheimer dominated the 96th Academy Awards and won the top prize.

timeline_description: A timeline of major milestones in the history of the Academy Awards, the leading annual film awards in the United States.
title: Anora wins Best Picture
description: Anora won Best Picture at the 97th Academy Awards, continuing the awards' evolving record into the mid-2020s.

description: Post-Apollo planning elevated the reusable shuttle as a core future NASA program.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Space Shuttle Program
timeline_description: A timeline of the planning, flights, disasters, achievements, and retirement of NASA's Space Shuttle program.
title: First Hubble servicing mission flies
description: Astronauts repaired Hubble, demonstrating the shuttle's unmatched on-orbit servicing role.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Space Shuttle Program

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Hong Xiuquan experiences religious visions
description: After failing the civil service examinations, Hong Xiuquan underwent visions that later shaped Taiping ideology.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Hong embraces his Christian-inspired mission
description: Hong concluded that he had a divine role and began preaching a heterodox religious message.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Society of God Worshippers expands
description: Hong and Feng Yunshan built a growing movement among Hakka communities in Guangxi.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Armed uprising begins in Guangxi
description: Open rebellion against Qing authority began as the Taiping movement militarized.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Hong proclaims the Heavenly Kingdom
description: Hong Xiuquan declared the Taiping Tianguo and assumed leadership as Heavenly King.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Taiping forces win at Yongan
description: Early victories helped turn the uprising into a full-scale revolutionary movement.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Taiping armies move north through the Yangtze region
description: The rebels broke out of Guangxi and advanced across south-central China.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Nanjing falls to the Taiping
description: The capture of Nanjing gave the Taiping a major city and future capital.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Nanjing is renamed Tianjing
description: The rebels made Nanjing their Heavenly Capital and administrative center.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Northern Expedition is launched
description: The Taiping leadership attempted an ambitious but ultimately unsuccessful drive toward Beijing.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Qing regional armies intensify resistance
description: Local military forces such as Xiang Army formations became central to the Qing counteroffensive.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Tianjing Incident devastates the leadership
description: A bloody internal purge destroyed much of the Taiping high command.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Taiping pressure on Shanghai grows
description: The conflict increasingly threatened treaty-port regions and foreign interests.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Taiping forces capture Suzhou and Hangzhou
description: Renewed campaigns briefly restored rebel momentum in the lower Yangtze.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Foreign-backed defense of Shanghai hardens
description: Western-supported local forces increasingly cooperated with the Qing against the Taiping.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Zeng Guofan tightens the siege of Nanjing
description: Qing commanders methodically constricted the Taiping capital.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Ever Victorious Army is reorganized
description: Foreign-led but Qing-aligned forces added pressure on Taiping positions around Shanghai.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Suzhou falls to Qing forces
description: The Qing recovery of Suzhou marked a major strategic reversal for the rebellion.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Hong Xiuquan dies
description: The Taiping leader died as Qing forces closed in on Tianjing.

timeline_description: A timeline of the origins, expansion, internal crises, and defeat of the Taiping Rebellion in Qing China.
title: Nanjing is recaptured by the Qing
description: The fall of Nanjing effectively ended the Taiping Rebellion as a major organized state.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.

description: Maxwell's theory established the scientific basis for radio communication.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.

description: Hertz experimentally confirmed the existence of electromagnetic waves.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Marconi begins wireless experiments
description: Guglielmo Marconi began building a practical wireless communication system.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Marconi files a wireless patent in Britain
description: The patent helped establish commercial wireless telegraphy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Marconi company is incorporated
description: Commercial development of radio equipment accelerated with Marconi's first company.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: First transatlantic wireless signal is reported

location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Marconi and Braun win the Nobel Prize in Physics
description: The Nobel recognized advances in wireless telegraphy and the practical development of radio.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Titanic disaster prompts radio regulation
description: The sinking highlighted the life-saving importance of wireless operators and radio standards.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: KDKA begins scheduled broadcasting
description: Station KDKA in Pittsburgh is widely regarded as pioneering regular commercial radio broadcasting.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: BBC is founded
description: The British Broadcasting Company was created, shaping public-service radio in the United Kingdom.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Radio Act creates the Federal Radio Commission
description: The United States established a stronger federal framework for licensing and spectrum control.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Edwin Armstrong demonstrates FM radio
description: Frequency modulation offered much greater resistance to static than AM broadcasting.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Federal Communications Commission is created
description: The FCC replaced the Federal Radio Commission and became the main U.S. communications regulator.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: War of the Worlds broadcast becomes famous
description: Orson Welles's Mercury Theatre broadcast became one of radio's best-known cultural moments.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Commercial FM broadcasting is authorized in the United States
description: FM entered regular U.S. service, opening a new phase in broadcasting.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Transistor is introduced into electronics
description: The transistor paved the way for smaller, cheaper, and more portable radios.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Regency TR-1 portable transistor radio goes on sale
description: The first commercially successful transistor radio transformed everyday listening.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Public Broadcasting Act creates NPR's framework
description: The act helped create the institutional basis for U.S. public radio.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of Radio
timeline_description: A timeline of the scientific discoveries, technical breakthroughs, and broadcasting milestones that shaped radio.
title: Digital radio standards begin to spread

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Achaemenes is remembered as the dynastic ancestor
description: Later Persian tradition treated Achaemenes as the eponymous founder of the royal house.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Teispes rules in Persis
description: Teispes was an early ruler from whom major Achaemenid lines later claimed descent.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Cyrus II becomes king of Anshan
description: Cyrus the Great began the career that would create the Persian Empire.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Cyrus defeats the Medes
description: Cyrus overthrew Astyages and established Achaemenid supremacy in Iran.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Lydia is conquered
description: The defeat of Croesus extended Persian rule into Anatolia.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Babylon falls to Cyrus
description: Persian conquest of Babylon made the Achaemenids the dominant power of the Near East.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Cambyses II succeeds Cyrus
description: Cambyses II inherited the empire after the death of Cyrus the Great.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Egypt is conquered
description: Cambyses II defeated Psamtik III and brought Egypt under Persian rule.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Darius I seizes power
description: After a succession crisis, Darius I established himself as king and restored order.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Construction begins at Persepolis
description: Darius I initiated the monumental royal complex at Persepolis.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Imperial administration is reorganized
description: Darius systematized satrapies, taxation, and roads across the empire.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Ionian Revolt begins
description: Greek cities in Asia Minor revolted against Persian rule, starting a long conflict with the Greek mainland.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Persians are defeated at Marathon
description: Darius's expedition into Greece suffered a setback at Marathon.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Xerxes I succeeds Darius I
description: Xerxes inherited the empire and prepared a massive campaign against Greece.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Thermopylae and Salamis shape the Greek war
description: Xerxes won at Thermopylae but suffered a major naval defeat at Salamis.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Persia is defeated at Plataea
description: Greek victory at Plataea ended Xerxes's invasion of mainland Greece.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Artaxerxes I becomes king
description: Artaxerxes I took the throne after the death of Xerxes I.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Artaxerxes II succeeds after Darius II
description: A new reign opened amid dynastic contest and western wars.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Darius III becomes king
description: Darius III inherited an empire facing the challenge of Macedonian expansion.

timeline_description: A timeline of the rulers, conquests, wars, and fall of the Achaemenid Empire in the ancient Near East.
title: Alexander conquers the empire
description: The defeat of Darius III and Alexander's advance ended Achaemenid imperial rule.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France

description: This marriage joined the Capetian line to Bourbon lands and underpinned the later royal house.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Louis I becomes duke of Bourbon
description: Louis I is recognized as the first duke and namesake of the Bourbon line.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Henry of Navarre becomes heir presumptive
description: The death of François, duke of Anjou, made the Bourbon prince the likely successor to the French throne.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Henry IV becomes the first Bourbon king of France
description: After the death of Henry III, Henry of Navarre succeeded and founded the Bourbon royal line in France.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Edict of Nantes is issued
description: Henry IV granted substantial rights to Protestants, stabilizing the kingdom after the Wars of Religion.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Henry IV is assassinated
description: The death of Henry IV ended the founding Bourbon reign and brought Louis XIII to the throne.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Louis XIII succeeds as king
description: Louis XIII began a reign later associated with the growing power of the centralized monarchy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Cardinal Richelieu enters the royal ministry
description: Richelieu became the dominant minister under Louis XIII and strengthened royal authority.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Louis XIV becomes king
description: Louis XIV inherited the throne and later embodied Bourbon absolutism.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: The Fronde begins
description: Civil unrest during Louis XIV's minority challenged royal authority.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Louis XIV begins personal rule
description: After Mazarin's death, Louis XIV governed without a chief minister.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Court settles permanently at Versailles
description: Versailles became the ceremonial and political center of Bourbon monarchy.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Edict of Nantes is revoked
description: Louis XIV revoked toleration for Protestants, a major decision of the Bourbon state.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Louis XV succeeds Louis XIV
description: The very long reign of Louis XIV ended and the young Louis XV became king.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Louis XVI becomes king
description: Louis XVI inherited the throne on the eve of financial and political crisis.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: French Revolution begins
description: The Bourbon monarchy entered a revolutionary crisis that would destroy the old regime.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Monarchy is abolished
description: The National Convention ended the Bourbon monarchy and proclaimed the republic.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: Bourbon Restoration begins
description: Louis XVIII returned to the throne after Napoleon's first abdication.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: July Revolution overthrows Charles X
description: The elder Bourbon line lost the throne during the July Revolution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Bourbon Dynasty in France
timeline_description: A timeline of the rise, rule, revolutions, restoration, and final fall of the Bourbon dynasty in France.
title: The last Bourbon-related French monarchy falls
description: The overthrow of Louis-Philippe ended the Orléanist monarchy and closed the Bourbon age in France.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Lübeck is refounded
description: The refoundation of Lübeck created the city that became the central hub of Hanseatic trade.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Merchants secure privileges at Visby
description: German merchants gained early commercial advantages in the Baltic trading world.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Cologne merchants obtain London privileges
description: Commercial rights in England strengthened the western reach of German merchant networks.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Lübeck and Hamburg conclude a key alliance
description: The agreement between Lübeck and Hamburg became a foundational moment in Hanseatic cooperation.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Merchant privileges are expanded in Novgorod
description: The eastern trade in wax, furs, and other goods became more firmly established.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: The Peterhof kontor operates in Novgorod
description: The Hanseatic trading post at Novgorod emerged as one of the league's key eastern stations.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: League arrangements take shape in northern Germany
description: By the late thirteenth century interurban cooperation was taking a more organized form.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Danish pressure under Valdemar IV intensifies
description: Conflict with Denmark pushed Hanseatic towns toward stronger collective action.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Confederation of Cologne is formed
description: Hanseatic cities allied against Denmark in one of the league's most important joint efforts.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Treaty of Stralsund confirms Hanseatic power
description: Victory over Denmark brought the league major commercial and political advantages.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: The Hanse reaches its classic peak
description: Dozens of towns and overseas kontors sustained the league's dominance in northern trade.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: War against Baltic piracy expands
description: Collective security against pirates remained a core function of Hanseatic cooperation.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Peace of Vordingborg reshapes Danish relations
description: A new settlement helped restore trade after renewed conflict in the Baltic.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: The Novgorod kontor is closed by Moscow
description: Muscovite expansion weakened the league's eastern commercial position.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: The Steelyard loses favor in England
description: Changing English policy eroded one of the league's most valuable western privileges.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: League influence contracts to fewer core cities
description: By the mid-sixteenth century the Hanse was no longer the dominant commercial force it had been.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: English privileges for Hanse merchants are ended
description: The closure of the London Steelyard symbolized the weakening of the league in western Europe.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Only a few cities still maintain the Hanse name
description: The league survived mainly as a reduced legal and diplomatic shell.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Last Hanseatic diet is held
description: The final Hansetag at Lübeck is generally treated as the league's end.

timeline_description: A timeline of the growth, dominance, conflicts, and decline of the Hanseatic League in northern Europe.
title: Legacy survives in the Free Hanseatic Cities
description: Cities such as Hamburg, Bremen, and Lübeck preserved Hanseatic identity into the modern era.
